hero.title: Energy & logistics
hero.lead: Distribution networks don't fail loudly. They erode quietly — through delayed escalations, informal workarounds, and coordination gaps that no individual can see from where they sit.

focus.title: The structural problems that define this industry
focus.description: Every distribution operation is different in its specifics. The structural problems are remarkably consistent. These are the ones we see most often — and have built systems to solve.

pillars.title: Multi-depot coordination
pillars.description: Status, accountability, and escalation break down when decisions need to cross depot boundaries. We design the coordination layer that holds it together at scale.

pillars.title: Exception visibility
pillars.description: In most operations, failures are invisible until they're escalated by a human. We design platforms where exceptions surface automatically — before they become expensive.

pillars.title: Real-time decision context
pillars.description: Dispatch and operations decisions are made on stale data. The fix isn't faster reporting — it's redesigning the information architecture that feeds those decisions.

pillars.title: Growth without fragmentation
pillars.description: New depots, new routes, new teams — these all stress coordination systems that weren't designed to absorb them. We build the infrastructure that makes expansion predictable.

caseStudies.title: Rebuilding the coordination layer for a 340-depot fuel distributor
caseStudies.result: On-time delivery: 84% → 98.3%. Issue resolution: 48 hrs → 2 hrs.

caseStudies.title: Fleet operations platform for enterprise logistics provider
caseStudies.result: 1,200+ vehicles. Real-time dispatch visibility built from scratch.

whitePapers.title: Designing for exception visibility in multi-depot operations
The accountability gap: why informal governance breaks at scale

cta.eyebrow: Work with us in this space
cta.title: If this is your industry, we know the terrain.
cta.description: We've built operational systems across fuel distribution, fleet management, and multi-site logistics. We don't need a briefing on how the industry works — we need to understand where your specific coordination is breaking down.
